pubmed_links: [Lam & Krasnewich (2021) PMM2-CDG](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/20301289/); [Serrano et al. (2015) Phosphomannomutase deficiency (PMM2-CDG): ataxia and cerebellar assessment](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26502900/); [Epifani et al. (2023) Untangling adaptive functioning of PMM2-CDG across age and its impact on parental stress](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38129426/)
summary: PMM2 encodes phosphomannomutase 2, and biallelic loss-of-function variants cause PMM2-CDG, the most common congenital disorder of glycosylation, presenting with intellectual disability (IQ 40–70), cerebellar ataxia, and global developmental delay; some patients additionally exhibit autism spectrum features and behavioral disturbances. (well-established)
